# OROSTONE — Full LLM Reference Document

> This is the complete, detailed reference for OROSTONE. For a concise summary, see: https://orostone.sk/llms.txt

## Company Overview

OROSTONE s.r.o. is a Slovak premium supplier of large-format sintered stone (veľkoformátové sinterované platne) for residential and commercial interiors. Founded and operating in Bratislava, Slovakia. We supply, cut, and deliver premium sintered stone slabs. We are not a manufacturer — we source from leading European producers and provide complete turnkey service.

**Legal details:**
- Company: OROSTONE s.r.o.
- Address: Landererova 8, 811 09 Bratislava, mestská časť Staré Mesto (note: registered office, not showroom)
- IČO: 55 254 772
- DIČ: 2121930580
- IČ DPH: SK2121930580 (VAT registered since 11 April 2023, §4)
- Registered: Obchodný register Mestského súdu Bratislava III, oddiel Sro, vložka 167404/B
- Email: info@orostone.sk
- Phone: +421 917 588 738
- Website: https://orostone.sk
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/orostone.sk/

---

## What Is Sintered Stone?

Sintered stone (sinterovaný kameň) is a premium surface material made from 100% natural minerals — clay, feldspar, silica, and natural pigments — compressed under 15,000 tonnes of pressure and fired at 1,200°C for 45 minutes. No resins, no artificial binders. The result is an ultra-dense, ultra-hard surface that mimics the appearance of natural stone (marble, travertine, concrete) while dramatically outperforming it in durability.

**Technical properties:**
- Porosity: < 0.1% (near-zero — stain-proof without sealing)
- Mohs hardness: 6–7 (diamond is 10, granite is 6–7)
- Heat resistance: up to 300°C continuous, 500°C short-term
- UV resistance: no fading outdoors or in bright light
- Flexural strength: up to 16,000N for 20mm slabs
- Chemical resistance: resistant to acids (lemon, vinegar), alkalis, solvents
- Frost resistance: suitable for exterior applications
- Food safety: certified for direct food contact

**Available thicknesses:**
- 3mm: wall cladding, furniture fronts, overlay
- 6mm: lightweight applications, furniture
- 12mm: standard countertops, floors, walls
- 20mm: premium worktops, outdoor, high-load floors

**Standard slab sizes:** 3200×1600mm, 3200×1440mm (varies by design)

**Finishes available:**
- Matte (matný): softer appearance, hides fingerprints, feels silky
- Polished (leštený): mirror-like, dramatic veining, shows fingerprints more
- Leather/Textured (štruktúrovaný): tactile surface, non-slip

---

## Product Catalog — Complete Specifications

All products are large-format sintered stone slabs (veľkoformátové platne). Standard size 3200×1600mm. Available in 3mm, 12mm, 20mm thicknesses unless noted.

### 01. STATUARIO DIAMANTE
- URL: https://orostone.sk/produkt/statuario-diamante
- Style: White base, dramatic silver-grey veining, Italian marble inspiration
- Best for: Luxury bathrooms, living rooms, entrance halls, kitchen backsplashes
- Finishes: Matte, Polished
- Price range: Mid-premium (€€€)
- SEO keywords: statuario diamante, biely mramor obklad, veľkoformátové platne bratislava, luxusné obklady, porcelánové platne

- Finishes: Polished (most popular), Matte

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### General Product FAQs

**Q: Is sintered stone suitable for bathrooms?**
A: Yes. Porcelain large-format slabs have near-zero porosity (< 0.1%), making them ideal for bathrooms, showers, and wellness spaces. No sealing required. They withstand permanent water contact without damage.

**Q: How do I care for the surface?**
A: Maintenance is minimal — warm water and mild soap is sufficient. The material is non-porous, requires no sealing or waxing. It resists common cleaning products including mild acids (lemon, vinegar).

**Q: Can I place a hot pot directly on the surface?**
A: Yes. Sintered stone withstands temperatures up to 300°C. You can place hot pots or pans directly on the surface without risk of damage or discolouration.

**Q: What is the delivery time?**
A: Stock slabs are typically delivered within 5 working days after payment confirmation. Custom projects (with cutting and edge profiling) are completed within 15 working days.

**Q: Do you provide installation?**
A: OROSTONE is a material supplier — we do not provide installation directly. We can recommend verified specialist installers experienced with large-format slabs upon request.

**Q: What is the price per m²?**
A: Prices vary by design and thickness. Indicative range: 12mm slabs from €85–€180/m² depending on design. 20mm premium slabs from €120–€240/m². Contact us for a precise quote.

**Q: Is sintered stone the same as Dekton or Neolith?**
A: Dekton (Cosentino) and Neolith are brand names for sintered stone — the same technology with different branding and proprietary formulations. OROSTONE sources from European producers offering equivalent technical performance.

**Q: Can sintered stone be used outdoors?**
A: Yes, some designs (e.g. Roman Travertine) are frost-resistant and rated for exterior use — terraces, balconies, pool surrounds. Confirm with us which specific design suits your outdoor project.

---

## The Orostone Process — 10 Steps

1. **Consultation** — Free initial call or showroom visit. We assess your project, space, and style preferences. Duration: 30–60 minutes.
2. **Material selection** — We bring physical samples of your shortlisted designs. You see and touch the actual material in your space.
3. **Digital laser templating** — Our technician visits your site with a Leica laser scanner. Precision: ±0.05mm. No room for measurement error.
4. **CNC design** — The digital template is imported into our CAD software. We design every cut, edge profile, and cutout (sink, hob, etc.) digitally.
5. **Material ordering** — We order your specific slab batch from the manufacturer. Typically 5–10 working days lead time.
6. **CNC fabrication** — Professional two-pass diamond-tool cutting on precision CNC machines. All edge profiles (half-bullnose, mitre, pencil) finished in our facility.
7. **Quality control** — Every piece inspected for chips, micro-cracks, colour consistency. Any issue caught before delivery.
8. **Transport** — In-house delivery in custom A-frame vehicles. Slabs travel vertically to prevent flexion damage. Crew of 2 minimum for handling.
9. **Installation** — Certified technicians handle substrate preparation, adhesive selection, levelling, and placement. Expansion joints where required.
10. **Final inspection** — Post-installation walkthrough with client. Minor adjustments, sealant application to joints where specified. Warranty documentation provided.

Total project timeline: 10–15 working days from approved template to completed installation.

---

## Shipping and Payment Information

- **Delivery area:** Slovak Republic (primary), Czech Republic (available on request)
- **Delivery method:** In-house A-frame vehicle transport — not courier/pallet delivery
- **Payment:** Bank transfer (IBAN provided on invoice), card payment available
- **Currency:** EUR
- **VAT:** 23% Slovak DPH included in all prices (IČ DPH: SK2121930580)
- **Minimum order:** No formal minimum — single slab orders accepted
- **Shipping cost:** Calculated individually based on project size and location

---

## Returns and Complaints (Reklamácie)

- **Return window:** 14 days for uncut/unmodified standard goods
- **Custom-cut material:** Cannot be returned (made-to-measure)
- **Damaged goods:** Report within 24 hours of delivery with photographic evidence
- **Warranty:** 24 months on material defects; installation warranty per contractor
- **Complaint process:** Email info@orostone.sk with order number, photos, and description

---

## Blog — Key Insights

### Sintered Stone Problems Nobody Tells You
URL: https://orostone.sk/blog/problemy-so-sinterovanym-kamenom
Key takeaways: Sintered stone is hard but brittle at unsupported edges. Minimum 5mm corner radius for all cutouts (sinks, hobs). Use two-pass cutting technique — never single-pass. 90° edges are more vulnerable than half-bullnose profiles. Transport must be vertical (A-frame) — horizontal transport causes flexion cracks. All installation issues stem from inadequate substrate or unsupported overhangs.

### Sintered Stone vs Quartz vs Ceramic vs Dekton
URL: https://orostone.sk/blog/sinterovany-kamen-vs-kvarcit-vs-keramika-vs-dekton
Key takeaways: Sintered stone has better UV stability than quartz (no yellowing outdoors). Better heat resistance than quartz or engineered stone. Thinner than granite at equal strength. Dekton is the same technology — different brand. Ceramics are cheaper but have lower flexural strength and smaller format options. Decision matrix: for outdoor → sintered stone or Dekton; for budget-conscious → ceramics; for maximum scratch resistance → granite or sintered stone.

### Matte vs Polished Surface
URL: https://orostone.sk/blog/matny-vs-leskly-povrch
Key takeaways: Polished shows dramatic veining but shows fingerprints and micro-scratches more. Matte is more forgiving in daily use, hides water marks, feels silky. Photogenic preference: polished looks better in daylight photography, matte looks more luxurious in warm evening light. Recommendation: matte for family kitchens, polished for feature walls and low-traffic bathrooms.

### From Measurement to Installation
URL: https://orostone.sk/blog/od-merania-po-instalaciu-proces-orostone
Key takeaways: Digital templating is non-negotiable for large-format slabs — manual measurement error causes irreversible waste. Substrate preparation (levelling, waterproofing membrane) is 80% of installation quality. Expansion joints prevent cracking under thermal movement. Full project in 10–15 working days.

### Is Sintered Stone Worth It?
URL: https://orostone.sk/blog/oplati-sa-sinterovany-kamen
Key takeaways: 25-year total cost of ownership analysis shows sintered stone at €95/m² installation price has lower long-term cost than laminate (needs replacement) or granite (sealing costs). ROI on property value: premium kitchen/bathroom finish returns 80–120% of material cost at resale. Best value scenario: Airbnb/rental property — durability eliminates replacement cycles.

---
